items.quote: We finally design agent pipelines the same way we design systems — on a canvas. Execution and approval gates live next to the graph, not in a separate console.
items.author: Priya Sharma

items.quote: Flowcharts stopped being a documentation chore. The canvas is fast enough that process owners keep diagrams current themselves.
items.author: Marcus Chen

items.quote: We started with flowcharts, then moved critical paths into agent workflows. Same product family — no tool sprawl.
items.author: Elena Kovács

items.question: What is Eleven Nodes today?
items.answer: Eleven Nodes is a canvas platform for agent workflow automation and advanced diagram generation. You design multi-agent systems diagrammatically, run them with human-in-the-loop controls, and create clear process diagrams — with more diagram and automation modules planned on the same foundation.

items.question: How do agent workflows work?
items.answer: Open Agent Workflow Automation, place agents and tools on the canvas, connect them with optional approval gates, then execute from the sidebar. The canvas highlights active agents and surfaces review steps when human approval is required.

items.question: Can I generate flowcharts as well as agent graphs?
items.answer: Yes. Diagram Generator is a dedicated workspace for process diagrams — decisions, subprocesses, and smart connectors — separate from agent orchestration but sharing the same canvas UX.

items.question: Will you add more tools later?
items.answer: Yes. Architecture, UML, ER, BPMN, and deeper AI generation are on the roadmap. The platform is config-driven so new tools can ship as modules without changing how you open, share, or collaborate on workspaces.

items.question: Is my data used to train models?
items.answer: No. Your workflows, diagrams, and prompts are not used for model training. Enterprise plans can add residency and retention controls as AI features expand.

items.question: Can I cancel anytime?
items.answer: Yes. Downgrade or cancel from billing anytime — your work stays accessible on the Free plan.
